brief_justification: Exact correct answer (Month 6) with valid month-by-month arithmetic; Derives unstable equilibrium at 18,750 and explains why population must decline from 10,000 — multi-level analysis; Explicitly sets an equilibrium stability check before simulating, naming the anti-pattern of assuming convergence; Two-pronged verification: analytic equilibrium derivation plus full tabular simulation; Correctly notes the quota is unchanged from original catch but does not enumerate confidence levels or conditions that would alter the conclusion; Notes the quota is ineffective and the real driver is 10,000 < 18,750, but does not systematically consider alternative interpretations or quota values; Complete step-by-step table with every intermediate value; reproducible in full; The injection's negative gate is about commons depletion assumptions — the agent avoids assuming sustainability but does not explicitly name the anti-pattern; Avoids resource-infinity assumption and first-order-only blindness by tracing extinction trajectory, with no suppressed failure modes evident; Topology specifies a commons/game-theory branching DAG; agent correctly treats this as a math problem and solves it linearly, with no branch structure mapped to the topology.
